channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Notre Dame gets late pick and field goal to make title game with 27-24 win over Penn State
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — A diving interception off an ill-advised pass set up Notre Dame's trip to play for the program's 12th national title after a thrill-a-minute 27-24 victory over Penn State on Thursday night in the Orange Bowl.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penn State QB Drew Allar's late interception helps seal Notre Dame's win in Orange Bowl
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Drew Allar walked to the Penn State sideline with his hands on the top of his head, seemingly aware of where the College Football Playoff semifinal was heading.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guerrero agrees to $28.5M and Valdez to $18M; Tucker tops arbitration 17 requests at $17.5M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and the Toronto Blue Jays avoided a salary arbitration hearing when the first baseman agreed Thursday to a $28.5 million, one-year contract on the day players and teams exchanged proposed figures.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Riley Leonard returns from injury to lead Notre Dame to national championship game in CFP win vs Penn State
item.description: Notre Dame is going to the first College Football Playoff National Championship after defeating Penn State 27-24 in the Orange Bowl on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders fire general manager Tom Telesco after just one year
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — The Las Vegas Raiders continued their housecleaning Thursday, firing general manager Tom Telesco just two days after dismissing coach Antonio Pierce. The moves took place after the Raiders went 4-13 this season.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Notre Dame QB Riley Leonard gets injury scare, returns to rally Irish into CFP championship game
item.description: Notre Dame quarterback Riley Leonard briefly went out of the game, then returned to find the end zone in the College Football Playoff semifinal against Penn State on Thursday night at the Orange Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former finalist Josiah Cox relishing opportunity to return with SDSU football
item.description: Cox had 35 tackles and two interceptions for the Aggies in his year in Las Cruces, including an impressive performance against ranked Texas A&M. Those skills were apparent to SDSU head coach Sean Lewis and a retooled defensive coaching staff.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Jets interview Lions DC Aaron Glenn for their coach vacancy and Rams exec Ray Farmer for the GM job
item.description: The New York Jets interviewed Detroit Lions defensive coordinator Aaron Glenn for the head coach job and Los Angeles Rams executive Ray Farmer for their general manager vacancy.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Waszak Jr., Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Guerrero agrees to $28.5M and Valdez to $18M; Tucker tops arbitration 17 requests at $17.5M
item.description: Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and the Toronto Blue Jays avoided a salary arbitration hearing when the first baseman agreed to a $28.5 million, one-year contract on the day players and teams exchanged proposed figures.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mets great David Wright offers advice to Pete Alonso as the first baseman remains unsigned in free agency
item.description: New York Mets great David Wright gave advice to free agent first basemen Pete Alonso during a press conference about Wright having his jersey number retired Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia star Carson Beck expected to explore transfer portal: report
item.description: Quarterback Carson Beck, who helped lead Georgia to the 2024 SEC title, appears to have interest in continuing his college football career — albeit somewhere other than Athens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Comedian Shane Gillis says Nick Saban ‘spazzed on me’ after ESPN on-air appearance
item.description: Shane Gillis, the famous comedian, revealed that legendary college football coach Nick Saban "spazzed on me" after making jokes that he had cheated while at Alabama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lakers head coach JJ Redick's house among thousands that burned down in Los Angeles wildfires: report
item.description: Los Angeles Lakers head coach JJ Redick is among thousands who lost their homes in the Los Angeles area's wildfires after evacuating his family Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Researchers confirm 'Music City Miracle' star Frank Wycheck had CTE
item.description: Frank Wycheck, the NFL tight end best known for throwing the lateral that started the “Music City Miracle” and launched the Tennessee Titans’ run to the franchise’s lone Super Bowl appearance, had stage III chronic traumatic encephalopathy when he died on Dec.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina lands top defensive transfer as rumors swirl around Bill Belichick's NFL interest: report
item.description: The North Carolina Tar Heels and Bill Belichick reportedly landed a top defensive transfer on Wednesday amid rumors of his interest in NFL openings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connor Bedard continues hot stretch, career-best point streak at 8 games as Blackhawks beat Avalanche
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor Bedard is on a hot streak, even if the Blackhawks haven't been too hot lately. Bedard continued his roll on Wednesday night with a goal and an assist, and this time it led to a win, as the Blackhawks beat the Colorado Avalanche, 3-1, at the United Center.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls fall to Pacers, 2-game winning streak snapped as longest homestand of season begins
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Bulls were looking to go into their longest homestand of the season on a three-game winning streak. Instead, they'll be trying to start a new one.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Grayson Murray memories at Sony Open remind Woodland of the value of support
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Gary Woodland was like so many other players when he arrived at the Sony Open. His thoughts immediately went to defending champion Grayson Murray, who openly shared his struggles with anxiety and addiction, and who took his own life four months after winning.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinner, Djokovic in opposite halves at the Australian Open, Sabalenka vs Stephens in 1st round
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Defending champion Jannik Sinner and 10-time Australian Open winner Novak Djokovic have landed in opposite sides of the draw for the season’s first major, ruling out a replay of last year’s semifinal match.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the numbers of Cleveland's 129-122 win over Oklahoma City
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The most-hyped game so far this season in the NBA didn't disappoint. Cleveland beat Oklahoma City 129-122 on Wednesday night, with the Cavaliers — now winners of 11 straight — pushing the NBA's best record to 32-4 and snapping the Thunder's 15-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cavaliers beat Thunder 129-122 to end OKC's 15-game winning streak in matchup of NBA's top teams
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Jarrett Allen scored 25 points, Evan Mobley added 21 and the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Oklahoma City's 15-game winning streak by beating the Thunder 129-122 on Wednesday night in a marquee meeting between the NBA's top two teams.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox sign former Seattle Mariners infielder
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago White Sox signed free agent infielder Josh Rojas Wednesday, according to a press release from the team. The White Sox agreed to terms on a one-year, $3.5-million contract with Rojas. Rojas spent the 2024 season with the Seattle Mariners, hitting .225/.304/.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: University City 61, Lincoln 59
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- What a game on the hardwood at University City in boys action with the Centurions beating Lincoln in double overtime 61-59. Dylan Griffin paced the Centurions with a game high 38 points.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Brayden Point scores with 52 seconds to play to lift Lightning to 3-2 win over Hurricanes
item.description: Brayden Point scored with 52 seconds to play and the Tampa Bay Lightning snapped a four-game skid with a 3-2 victory over the Carolina Hurricanes on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Peter Seidler's brother responds to lawsuit over control of Padres
item.description: Matt Seidler, the brother of late Padres owner Peter Seidler, has responded to the lawsuit filed by Peter Seidler's widow over control of the team.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5

item.title: LIV Golf fills out its 2025 schedule with final 3 events going head-to-head with PGA Tour
item.description: LIV Golf will play its final three events in the United States the same week as the FedEx Cup playoffs on the PGA Tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CMU Women's wrestler Marissa Gallegos reflects on wrestling journey
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Marissa Gallegos is no stranger to the spotlight. The Colorado Mesa University star has cemented her legacy as the winningest women’s wrestler in program history. Her career, defined by grit, dominance, and determination, has inspired athletes across the sport.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Utah State cracks the top-25 rankings
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a 14-1 start, the Utah State basketball team is back in the top-25. The Aggies check in at No. 25 in both the AP Top 25 and USA Today Coaches Poll. Utah State is 4-0 in Mountain West play, under first-year USU head coach Jerrod Calhoun.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Final field for 2025 Farmers Insurance Open in San Diego shaping up
item.description: The 2025 Farmers Insurance Open is set to take place in San Diego from January 22-25, 2025, featuring a field of major championship winners, past winners, and players with San Diego ties. Tickets went on sale in October.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Buccaneers beat Saints 27-19 to clinch NFC South and wide receiver Mike Evans surpasses 1,000 yards
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Just 36 seconds away from clinching the NFC South title, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers had another goal to achieve. Take a knee on their 32-yard line and run out the clock? No chance. Mike Evans still needed 5 yards to make history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears vs. Packers: Injuries, inactives, matchups and what to know for the 2024 season finale
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Packers have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See below. GREEN BAY, Wis. — A long, trying 2024 season for the Bears reaches its merciful conclusion Sunday afternoon at Lambeau Field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Runnin' Utes get run over by Texas Tech, 93-65
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It may be a long first season in the Big 12 Conference for the Utes basketball team. Darrion Williams scored 19 points with nine assists, Federiko Federiko added 14 points on 7-of-9 shooting — including five dunks — on Saturday, and Texas Tech beat Utah 93-65.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Rocks cruise past Utah State in season opener
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The fifth-ranked Utah gymnastics team kicked off its 50th anniversary season in style, overcoming a slow start to eventually secure a dominant victory over Utah State Friday night at the Huntsman Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Naval Base San Diego basketball introduces two teams
item.description: The Naval Base San Diego basketball team is led by LaQuan Hansley, the former Triton player-turned-head coach, who revived the program after it was shut down during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5
